text: Photography and videography is a booming service business in Uganda, driven by weddings, corporate events, social media content, and real estate marketing. Two startup paths: 1) Smartphone photography — UGX 500,000-1,000,000 startup: good smartphone with a quality camera (many youth already have this), ring light (UGX 50,000-100,000), tripod with phone mount (UGX 30,000-80,000), portable backdrop (UGX 50,000-100,000), and editing apps (Lightroom Mobile, Snapseed, CapCut — all free). 2) Professional camera setup — UGX 1,500,000-3,000,000: second-hand DSLR/mirrorless camera like Canon 700D or Nikon D3400 (UGX 800,000-1,500,000 used from Kampala camera dealers), 50mm f/1.8 lens (UGX 300,000-500,000, the best value portrait lens), external flash (UGX 150,000-300,000), memory cards (UGX 30,000-60,000), camera bag (UGX 50,000-100,000), and editing software (Adobe Lightroom UGX 30,000/month or free alternatives like GIMP/Darktable). Location: work can be done on-location (events, outdoor shoots) or from a simple home studio setup.

Photography services and pricing in Uganda (April 2026): Event photography (weddings, graduations, parties): UGX 200,000-500,000 per event (4-8 hours shooting, 50-200 edited photos delivered digitally). Corporate events and conferences: UGX 300,000-800,000. Product photography (for e-commerce, catalogues): UGX 50,000-150,000 per session (10-30 products). Real estate photography: UGX 100,000-300,000 per property (interior + exterior + drone shots command premium). Passport and ID photos: UGX 5,000-10,000 per set (volume business near government offices). Portrait sessions (family, couple, maternity): UGX 100,000-250,000. Video production (social media ads, promos): UGX 200,000-1,000,000 depending on complexity. Monthly revenue potential: UGX 500,000-2,000,000 for part-time, UGX 2,000,000-5,000,000 for full-time with strong marketing. Peak seasons: November-February (wedding season, graduation ceremonies, Christmas events) and July-August (mid-year graduations).

Building your photography portfolio and finding clients: 1) Start with free/discounted shoots — photograph 5-10 events for free or at heavy discount to build a portfolio. Every professional photographer started this way. 2) Social media is your storefront — post every shoot on Instagram, TikTok, and WhatsApp Status. Use location tags and relevant hashtags (#KampalaPhotographer #UgandaWeddings #EventPhotographyUG). Before/after editing reels get massive engagement. 3) Network with event planners, MCs, DJs, and venue owners — they refer clients to you and you refer to them. Offer them a 10% referral commission. 4) Partner with hotels and restaurants — offer to photograph their food and spaces for their social media in exchange for referrals. 5) List on Google Business — many clients search for 'photographer near me' on Google. A free Google Business listing with photos and reviews drives enquiries. 6) WhatsApp Business catalogue — list your packages with sample images and prices. Share your catalogue link everywhere. 7) Approach businesses directly — walk into shops, restaurants, and salons. Offer product photography packages. Most small businesses in Uganda have terrible product photos — you are solving a real problem.

Growing from photographer to content creation agency: 1) Build a studio — once you consistently earn UGX 1,000,000+/month, invest in a small studio space (UGX 200,000-400,000/month rent). Add backdrops, lighting kits, and props. A studio enables passport photos, product shoots, and portrait sessions year-round regardless of weather. 2) Train and hire — take on 1-2 apprentices. Teach them your style and workflows. Send them to handle smaller jobs while you focus on premium clients. 3) Content creation packages — offer businesses monthly content packages: 4 social media posts/week + 2 reels/videos + story content for UGX 500,000-1,500,000/month per client. 5 clients = UGX 2,500,000-7,500,000/month. 4) Teaching and workshops — run photography workshops for beginners. Charge UGX 100,000-200,000 per participant. 10 participants per monthly workshop = UGX 1,000,000-2,000,000 extra income. 5) Print services — partner with printing companies to offer canvas prints, photo books, and framed portraits. Add 30-50% markup. 6) Drone photography — invest in a DJI Mini drone (UGX 1,500,000-3,000,000) for aerial shots. Real estate, construction, and tourism clients pay premium rates (UGX 200,000-500,000 per session) for aerial photography.
